param: calibration master flat for the set-03 full-depth groups render (per-frame division at calibrate)
values: skyflat361_set03 (set-03's own 361 lights, dark-subtracted, winsorized rejection — the ratified item-5 tightening)
hypothesis: a sky flat lives in sensor coordinates (vignetting/motes/PRNU are optics+sensor-fixed and neither was touched between re-aims), so the own-flat render differs immaterially (NULL) on linear regional medians, seqtilt, SPCC K and the finals; a MATERIAL delta implicates the shared-flat reuse (removal-register row) as a live candidate for the multi-set combine's washout/gradient OPEN flaw (BACKLOG item 8). DECLARED second difference: the candidate flat also carries the median->winsorized rejection tightening (BACKLOG item 5); if a delta appears, attribution = flat-vs-flat ratio stat + a --rej=median own-lights rebuild, one knob from there
instrument: identical route + driver (run_undistort_groups.sh: 25 groups 11x15+14x14, in-group rej w 3 3, plain-mean compose, keep-all recipe) with ONLY --flat= swapped; control sub-stacks preserved at work/groups_set-03_flat373; flat pinned+validated by scripts/stack/build_sky_flat.sh (chunk=19)
measures: the flat builder's validation record (regional stat, findstar speck count, autostretch preview) vs skyflat_qa.json's numbers; siril stat regional medians (LINEAR domain) on both _spcc stacks; scripts/qa/star_shape.py seqtilt on the candidate stack vs star_shape_set03full.json; SPCC K factors both arms; finals = the user's eyes on judge/set-03_full_spcc-linked.png vs judge/set-03_ownflat_spcc-linked.png (full-frame lossless PNG16, like encodings; dust preservation + gradient the criteria)

verdict: win (objective measures); adoption + aesthetic/dust gate = the user's eyes on the judgment pair

measured.flat_validation: skyflat361_set03: centre 128 ADU vs corners 57-70 (corner/centre 0.45-0.55, the lens's ~1 EV falloff; same shape as skyflat373's light-domain figures) - sensor content transfers; specks 101 (373-flat, median) -> 0 (winsorized)

seqtilt.note: canvases differ ~0.6% (per-run register reference); per-Mpx stars 918 -> 994 (+8%)

mechanism: the sky flat's LOW-ORDER component carries the source set's own residual sky gradient; dividing set-03's lights by set-01's flat IMPRINTS set-01's gradient (the measured +/-6% L-R tilt), while the own flat's low-order term matches and cancels set-03's - the sensor-fixed content (vignetting/motes/PRNU) transfers (flat shapes agree), the sky-gradient term does NOT. First controlled evidence for the shared-flat candidate in the combine's gradient flaw (BACKLOG item 8); the washout/streak components remain open
inspection_notes: assembler 1:1 (right/centre zones, matched crops results/july14/inspect_set-03_ownflat_pair/): control right side sunk with suppressed faint stars; candidate even edge-to-edge, deeper faint field, dark-dust lanes stand out as structure; NO drift streaks, NO carved cores, NO chroma blotch in either arm at inspected zones. Whole-frame: dust/IFN MORE visible in candidate, not erased - the dust gate question for the user is whether any largest-scale IFN ramp was absorbed by the matched low-order term
declared_confounds: flat rejection differs with the source (median control-flat vs winsorized candidate-flat, the ratified item-5 tightening); pointlike specks cannot produce a corner-scale 13-ADU tilt, so the gradient delta attributes to the low-order term; if attribution is ever needed: flat-vs-flat ratio stat + a --rej=median own-lights rebuild. Canvas differs ~0.6% between arms (independent register references)
artifacts: results/july14/judge/set-03_full_spcc-linked.png (control) vs judge/set-03_ownflat_spcc-linked.png (candidate), full-frame lossless PNG16, like encodings; stacks results/july14/stack_set-03_{full,ownflat}[_wcs|_spcc].fit; records qa_work/{skyflat361_set03_qa,gradient_stat_set03full,gradient_stat_set03ownflat,star_shape_set03ownflat}.json + work/spcc_set-03_set-03_ownflat.json; control sub-stacks preserved work/groups_set-03_flat373/, candidate work/groups_set-03/
arm_rig_caveat: measured on the arm base rig - a method-validation A/B; re-measure on x86 per the migration rule before production adoption there

amendment: ADOPTED — the user ratified the per-set flat rule: a flat calibrates only the exact frames it was built from; cross-set reuse and shared/union combine flats are banned (docs/dead-ends.md imprint entry, README row 1b, BACKLOG register + items 5/8). stack_set-03_ownflat is set-03's render. Cleanup under the rule removed the wrong-flat products (all multi-set combine stacks/renders/records, stack_set-02_full*, groups_set-02, and the control's sub-stacks groups_set-03_flat373); the control stack + judge PNG are RETAINED as this experiment's preserved artifact pair.
